name: Sneak into basket
summary: Items the user did not select are added to the cart automatically, often with a pre-checked checkbox that must be unchecked to remove.

evidence.claim: Brignull's canonical taxonomy lists 'sneak into basket' as surreptitious cart additions.

body.description: An item the user did not explicitly add appears in the cart. Typically uses a pre-checked 'add extended warranty' or 'add protection plan' checkbox on the product page, or an auto-added 'recommended' accessory in the cart (E1).

detectionHeuristics.description: Pre-checked checkboxes at the moment of cart addition.
Cart count rises between product-add events without corresponding user add-to-cart clicks.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: You are evaluating a cart page. Compare the cart contents to the products the user clicked to add. For each cart line item, classify as 'user-added', 'sneak-into-basket' (added without explicit click), or 'optional-accessory' (user opted in). For sneak-into-basket items, return the item name and dollar amount.
body.remediation: 1. Highlight the surreptitious cart addition. 2. One-click to remove. 3. Warn the user that this vendor uses sneak-into-basket.
